an10.108:1.1: “Monks, doctors give a purgative for warding off diseases caused by bile, diseases caused by phlegm, diseases caused by the internal wind property.
an10.108:1.2: There is a purging there; I don’t say that there’s not,
an10.108:1.3: but it sometimes succeeds and sometimes fails.
an10.108:2.1: So I will teach you the noble purgative that always succeeds and never fails, a purgative whereby beings subject to birth are freed from birth; beings subject to aging are freed from aging; beings subject to death are freed from death; beings subject to sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress & despair are freed from sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress & despair.
an10.108:2.2: Listen & pay close attention. I will speak.”
an10.108:2.3: “As you say, lord,” the monks responded to him.
an10.108:3.1: “Now, what is the noble purgative that always succeeds and never fails, a purgative whereby beings subject to birth are freed from birth; beings subject to aging are freed from aging; beings subject to death are freed from death; beings subject to sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress & despair are freed from sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress & despair?
an10.108:4.1: “In one who has right view, wrong view is purged away,
an10.108:4.2: and the many evil, unskillful mental qualities that come into play in dependence on wrong view are purged away as well,
an10.108:4.3: while the many skillful mental qualities that depend on right view go to the culmination of their development.
an10.108:5.1: “In one who has right resolve, wrong resolve is purged away.…
an10.108:5.2: “In one who has right speech, wrong speech is purged away.…
an10.108:5.3: “In one who has right action, wrong action is purged away.…
an10.108:5.4: “In one who has right livelihood, wrong livelihood is purged away.…
an10.108:5.5: “In one who has right effort, wrong effort is purged away.…
an10.108:5.6: “In one who has right mindfulness, wrong mindfulness is purged away.…
an10.108:5.7: “In one who has right concentration, wrong concentration is purged away.…
an10.108:5.8: “In one who has right knowledge, wrong knowledge is purged away.…
an10.108:6.1: “In one who has right release, wrong release is purged away,
an10.108:6.2: and the many evil, unskillful mental qualities that come into play in dependence on wrong release are purged away as well,
an10.108:6.3: while the many skillful mental qualities that depend on right release go to the culmination of their development.
an10.108:6.4: “This, monks, is the noble purgative that always succeeds and never fails, a purgative whereby beings subject to birth are freed from birth; beings subject to aging are freed from aging; beings subject to death are freed from death; beings subject to sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress & despair are freed from sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress & despair.”
